311	/-! ## Gluing a sheaf of modules along a scheme glue datum
312	
313	`Scheme.Modules.glue` descends a sheaf of modules from per-chart data plus a transition
314	cocycle over a `Scheme.GlueData`. Mathlib carries no turn-key module descent over a
315	scheme glue datum (confirmed), so this is an Archon-original construction. The
316	construction path (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue`): restrict to a chart through the
317	open-immersion pullback equivalence (`Scheme.Modules.overRestrictPullbackIso`) and glue
318	the local sections by locality of sections (`existsUnique_gluing'`).
319	
320	NOTE (scaffold): the body and the module-cocycle hypotheses on `g` are still to be filled;
321	the transition data `g` (per-overlap pullback isos) is recorded in the signature, the
322	multiplicative cocycle conditions remain to be added before the construction is closed. -/
323	
324	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
325	
326	/-! ### Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism to a triple overlap
327	
328	To state the triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) of a module descent datum we must
329	transport each transition isomorphism `g_ij`, living on the overlap `V_ij`, to the common
330	triple overlap `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik`. The transport pulls `g_ij` back along a
331	projection `p : V_ijk ⟶ V_ij` and reassociates the iterated pullbacks via the pseudofunctor
332	comparison `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`. The three scheme-level `glueData_bridge_*`
333	identities below (consequences of `t_fac`, `pullback.condition` and `cocycle`) line up the
334	endpoints of the three transports so that the cocycle equation is well typed. -/
335	
336	/-- **Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism along a morphism**
337	(`lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`). Given a transition isomorphism
338	`g : a^*Mᵢ ≅ b^*Mⱼ` over `V` and a morphism `p : W ⟶ V`, transport it to `W` as an
339	isomorphism `(p ≫ a)^*Mᵢ ≅ (p ≫ b)^*Mⱼ`, by pulling `g` back along `p` and reassociating
340	the iterated pullbacks through `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`.
341	
342	Project-local: this is the pullback-pseudofunctor packaging that lets the three transition
343	isomorphisms attached to a triple of charts be compared on a single triple overlap; Mathlib
344	has no descent-of-modules-over-a-scheme-glue-datum API. -/
345	noncomputable def pullbackBaseChangeTransport {W V : Scheme.{u}} (p : W ⟶ V)
346	    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{u}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj)
347	    {Mi : Yi.Modules} {Mj : Yj.Modules}
348	    (g : (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj Mj) :
349	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ a)).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ b)).obj Mj :=
350	  (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app Mi ≪≫
351	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso g ≪≫
352	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app Mj
353	
354	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (source): on `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the two projections to
355	`V_ij` and `V_ik` followed by the overlap immersions `f_ij`, `f_ik` agree as morphisms to
356	`U_i`. This is the pullback condition; it identifies the sources of the `ij`- and
357	`ik`-transports. Project-local helper for the module cocycle (C2). -/
358	theorem glueData_bridge_src (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
359	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i j
360	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i k := pullback.condition
361	
362	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (middle): the `ij`-transition's target leg
363	`p^{ij} ≫ (t_ij ≫ f_ji)` to `U_j` coincides with the `jk`-transition's source leg
364	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ f_jk`. Follows from `t_fac` and the pullback condition; it identifies
365	the target of the `ij`-transport with the source of the `jk`-transport. Project-local helper
366	for the module cocycle (C2). -/
367	theorem glueData_bridge_mid (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
368	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)
369	      = (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ D.f j k := by
370	  rw [Category.assoc, pullback.condition, ← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc, D.t_fac i j k,
371	    Category.assoc]
372	
373	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (target): the `jk`-transition's target leg
374	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ (t_jk ≫ f_kj)` to `U_k` coincides with the `ik`-transition's target
375	leg `p^{ik} ≫ (t_ik ≫ f_ki)`. This is the heart of the cocycle, derived from `t_fac`, the
376	pullback condition, `t_inv` and `cocycle`; it identifies the target of the composite
377	`jk`-after-`ij` transport with the target of the `ik`-transport. Project-local helper for
378	the module cocycle (C2). -/
379	theorem glueData_bridge_tgt (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
380	    (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j)
381	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) := by
382	  have key : pullback.fst (D.f k i) (D.f k j) ≫ D.f k i
383	      = D.t' k i j ≫ pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.t i k ≫ D.f k i := by
384	    rw [D.t_fac_assoc k i j, ← Category.assoc (D.t k i) (D.t i k), D.t_inv, Category.id_comp]
385	  rw [Category.assoc, ← D.t_fac_assoc j k i,
386	    ← @pullback.condition _ _ _ _ _ (D.f k i) (D.f k j) _, key, D.cocycle_assoc i j k]
387	
388	/-- **Gluing a sheaf of modules along an open cover given by a scheme glue datum**
389	(`def:scheme_modules_glue`). From a glue datum `D`, per-chart sheaves of modules `M i`,
390	and transition isomorphisms `g i j` comparing the two charts' sheaves over the overlap
391	`V (i,j)` (after pullback), produces a glued sheaf of `O_{D.glued}`-modules.
392	
393	Project-local: Mathlib has no module descent over a scheme glue datum. -/
394	noncomputable def glue (D : Scheme.GlueData)
395	    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
396	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
397	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
398	    -- (C1) self-identity: over the diagonal overlap `V (i,i)` (where `t i i = 𝟙`) the
399	    -- transition isomorphism is the identity, i.e. the canonical isomorphism induced by
400	    -- `f i i = t i i ≫ f i i` (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C1)).
401	    (_hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
402	        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
403	    -- (C2) triple-overlap multiplicativity: over each triple overlap
404	    -- `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the base-change transports
405	    -- (`pullbackBaseChangeTransport`) of the three transition isomorphisms `g_ij`, `g_jk`,
406	    -- `g_ik` satisfy `ĝ_jk ∘ ĝ_ij = ĝ_ik`. The three `glueData_bridge_*` identities, applied
407	    -- through `pullbackCongr`, line up the endpoints so the equation is well typed
408	    -- (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C2), `lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`).
409	    (_hC2 : ∀ i j k,
410	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
411	            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
412	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
413	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
414	            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
415	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
416	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
417	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
418	            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k)) :
419	    D.glued.Modules :=
420	  -- **Effective descent as an equalizer of pushforwards.** The glued sheaf is the
421	  -- equalizer of the two canonical maps `∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ ⇉ ∏_{ij} (j_ij)_* (f_ij^* Mᵢ)`
422	  -- (`j_ij = f_ij ≫ ιᵢ : V_ij ↪ X`): the first map restricts the `i`-th chart section to
423	  -- `V_ij`, the second restricts the `j`-th and transports it across the transition `g_ij`,
424	  -- using the glue condition `(t_ij ≫ f_ji) ≫ ιⱼ = f_ij ≫ ιᵢ`. The cocycle hypotheses
425	  -- `_hC1`/`_hC2` are not needed to *construct* the object (they pin down the chart
426	  -- restriction isomorphisms `glueRestrictionIso`, built downstream). Pushforward preserves
427	  -- the sheaf condition and limits, so this equalizer of sheaves of modules is again a sheaf.
428	  let Qfun : D.J × D.J → D.glued.Modules := fun p =>
429	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1)).obj
430	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
431	  let P : D.glued.Modules := ∏ᶜ fun i => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i)
432	  -- first leg: restrict the `p.1`-chart section to the overlap `V (p.1, p.2)`
433	  let aComp : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
434	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).obj (M p.1) ⟶ Qfun p := fun p =>
435	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
436	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
437	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
438	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
439	  -- second leg: restrict the `p.2`-chart section, transport it across `g`, and reindex
440	  -- the immersion via the glue condition
441	  let bComp : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
442	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).obj (M p.2) ⟶ Qfun p := fun p =>
443	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
444	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
445	          (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
446	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
447	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
448	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
449	        ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
450	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
451	        (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
452	          rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
453	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
454	  let a : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.1 ≫ aComp p
455	  let b : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.2 ≫ bComp p
456	  equalizer a b
457	
458	/-! ### Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary scheme morphism
459	
460	The functor of points (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`) acts on morphisms by
461	pullback, and for that one needs `f^* (O^n) ≅ O^n` for an *arbitrary* scheme morphism
462	`f`. Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` supplies this only when the underlying
463	site functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`; the previous chapters discharged that `Final`
464	hypothesis only for open immersions and isomorphisms. The first lemma below removes the
465	restriction entirely: `Opens.map f.base` is `Final` for *every* scheme morphism, because
466	the structured-arrow category over any open `V` has a terminal object (the whole space
467	`⊤`). With that in hand, `pullbackFreeIso` and `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` hold for all
468	morphisms. -/
469	
470	/-- For an arbitrary scheme morphism `φ`, the site functor `Opens.map φ.base` is `Final`:
471	over any open `V` of the target the structured-arrow category `{U : V ≤ φ⁻¹ U}` has the
472	terminal object `U = ⊤`, hence is connected. This is the missing ingredient that makes
473	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit`/`pullbackObjFreeIso` applicable to *every* morphism,
474	not just to open immersions. Project-local. -/
475	lemma opensMap_final {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) :
476	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base).Final := by
477	  constructor
478	  intro V
479	  set top : StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base) :=
480	    StructuredArrow.mk (Y := (⊤ : T.Opens)) (homOfLE le_top)
481	  haveI : Nonempty (StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base)) := ⟨top⟩
482	  apply zigzag_isConnected
483	  intro s t
484	  have hs : s ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
485	  have ht : t ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
486	  exact Relation.ReflTransGen.trans
487	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inl ⟨hs⟩))
488	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inr ⟨ht⟩))
489	
490	/-- **Pullback of a free sheaf of modules is free, for any scheme morphism**: for
491	`φ : T' ⟶ T` and an index type `I`, `φ^*(O_T^{⊕I}) ≅ O_{T'}^{⊕I}`. Built from
492	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` once `opensMap_final` supplies the `Final` instance.
493	Project-local. -/
494	noncomputable def pullbackFreeIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) (I : Type u) :
495	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)
496	      ≅ SheafOfModules.free (R := T'.ringCatSheaf) I := by
497	  haveI := opensMap_final φ
498	  exact SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso φ.toRingCatSheafHom I
499	
500	/-- The free-pullback comparison is natural in the base morphism: equal morphisms give
501	`pullbackFreeIso`s related by the `eqToHom` transport of their (differing) sources.
502	Project-local — used for the bundle-transition self-identity. -/
503	lemma pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) (I : Type u) :
504	    eqToHom (congrArg
505	        (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)) h)
506	        ≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).hom
507	      = (pullbackFreeIso φ I).hom := by
508	  subst h; simp
509	
510	/-- Iso-level free-pullback cancellation: for equal base morphisms `φ = ψ`, the composite
511	`pullbackFreeIso φ ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ).symm` is the `eqToIso` transport between the
512	(differing) pullback sources. Proved generically (`φ`, `ψ` variables, `subst`), so applying
513	it never forces the kernel to whnf a concrete immersion — the leaner replacement for the
514	`.hom`-level cast chain in `bundleTransition_self`. Project-local. -/
515	lemma pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ)
516	    (I : Type u) :
517	    pullbackFreeIso φ I ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).symm
518	      = eqToIso (congrArg
519	          (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I))
520	          h) := by
521	  subst h; simp
522	
523	/-- **Pullback preserves rank-`d` local freeness.** If `M` is locally free of rank `d` on
524	`T`, then `φ^* M` is locally free of rank `d` on `T'`, for any scheme morphism `φ`. The
525	chart cover `{U i}` of `T` trivialising `M` pulls back to the cover `{φ⁻¹ U i}` of `T'`;
526	on each member the restriction of `φ^* M` is identified with the pulled-back chart-free
527	sheaf via the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp`, the factorisation
528	`φ ∘ (φ⁻¹ U i).ι = (φ ∣_ U i) ≫ (U i).ι` (`morphismRestrict_ι`), and `pullbackFreeIso`.
529	Project-local. -/
530	lemma pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) {M : T.Modules}
531	    {d : ℕ} (h : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank M d) :
532	    SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj M) d := by
533	  obtain ⟨ι, U, hcover, hloc⟩ := h
534	  refine ⟨ι, fun i => φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i), Scheme.Hom.iSup_preimage_eq_top φ hcover, ?_⟩
535	  intro i
536	  obtain ⟨e⟩ := hloc i
537	  exact ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i)).ι φ).app M ≪≫
538	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (morphismRestrict_ι φ (U i)).symm).app M ≪≫
539	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ∣_ (U i)) (U i).ι).app M).symm ≪≫
540	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (φ ∣_ (U i))).mapIso e ≪≫
541	    pullbackFreeIso (φ ∣_ (U i)) (ULift.{u} (Fin d))⟩
542	
543	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
544	
545	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
546	
547	/-! ## The GL_d bundle transition cocycle
548	
549	The universal quotient `U` is glued from the per-chart free rank-`d` sheaves `O_{U^I}^d`
550	along the bundle transitions `g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)⁻¹`, realised as matrix automorphisms via
551	`matrixToFreeIso` and conjugated to the overlap pullbacks by `pullbackFreeIso`. This section
552	constructs `bundleTransition` and proves its self-identity (C1); the triple-overlap
553	multiplicativity (C2) is the matrix cocycle of `lem:gr_cocycle` transported to the common
554	overlap by `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`/`glueData_bridge_*`. -/
555	
556	/-- The Cramer inverse of the self-minor `X^I_I` is the identity: since `X^I_I = 1`
557	(`universalMatrix_submatrix_self`) its inverse is `1`. Project-local; underlies C1. -/
558	lemma universalMinorInv_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
559	    universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
560	  have hmin : universalMinor d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
561	    rw [universalMinor, universalMatrix_submatrix_self, Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _)]
562	  rw [universalMinorInv, hmin, inv_one]
563	
564	/-- The injected Cramer inverse and minor matrices over the overlap structure sheaf are
565	mutually inverse — the `GL_d` invertibility hypotheses for `matrixToFreeIso`. Project-local. -/
566	private lemma bundleMatrix_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
567	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
568	    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
569	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
570	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ)) *
571	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
572	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
573	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)) = 1 ∧
574	    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
575	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
576	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)) *
577	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
578	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
579	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ)) = 1 := by
580	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
581	  · rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, map_one]
582	  · rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).2, map_one]
583	
584	/-- The **bundle transition** `g_{I,J}` (`def:gr_bundleTransition`): the isomorphism of
585	sheaves of modules on the overlap `U^I_J` induced by the invertible matrix
586	`(X^I_J)⁻¹ = universalMinorInv d r I J`. It identifies the pullback of `O_{U^I}^d` along
587	`f_{IJ}` with the pullback of `O_{U^J}^d` along `t_{IJ} ≫ f_{JI}`, by conjugating the
588	matrix automorphism `matrixToFreeIso (X^I_J)⁻¹` (built like `chartQuotientMap`) by the
589	free-pullback comparisons `pullbackFreeIso`. -/
590	noncomputable def bundleTransition (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r))
591	    (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
592	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ)).obj
593	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) ≅
594	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartTransition d r I J hI hJ ≫ chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)).obj
595	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r J).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :=
596	  Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (Fin d) ≪≫
597	    matrixToFreeIso
598	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
599	          (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
600	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ))
601	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
602	          (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
603	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ))
604	      (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1
605	      (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I J hI hJ).2 ≪≫
606	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso
607	      (chartTransition d r I J hI hJ ≫ chartIncl d r J I hJ hI) (Fin d)).symm
608	
609	/-- **Self-identity of the bundle transition (C1)** (`lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id`): on the
610	diagonal overlap `U^I_I` (where `t_{II} = 𝟙`) the bundle transition is the identity, in the
611	form required by the gluing primitive `Scheme.Modules.glue`. The matrix part is the identity
612	since `(X^I_I)⁻¹ = 1` (`universalMinorInv_self`), so `matrixEnd 1 = 𝟙` (`matrixEnd_one`); the
613	two free-pullback comparisons then cancel into the `eqToIso` transport.
614	
615	Resource note (iter-060): the former `set_option maxHeartbeats 1000000 in` override is
616	removed and the proof rebuilt as a *leaner term* that the kernel checks within the default
617	budget (the earlier `.hom`-level cast chain hit a `(kernel) deterministic timeout` at default
618	heartbeats and an OOM ceiling on cold builds at `1000000`). The new term works at the **iso
619	level**: the matrix automorphism is collapsed to `Iso.refl` in the lightweight single-overlap
620	context (`hB`, free sheaves only — no pullback), and the two free-pullback comparisons cancel
621	through the *generic* lemma `pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso` (proved by `subst` on
622	variable morphisms), so the kernel never whnfs the concrete immersions `chartIncl` /
623	`chartTransition`. -/
624	theorem bundleTransition_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
625	    bundleTransition d r I I hI hI
626	      = eqToIso (congrArg
627	          (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj
628	            (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)))
629	          (show chartIncl d r I I hI hI
630	              = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI from by
631	            rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp])) := by
632	  have hφ : chartIncl d r I I hI hI
633	      = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI := by
634	    rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp]
635	  -- The matrix automorphism is the identity iso: `(X^I_I)⁻¹` injects to the identity matrix,
636	  -- so its `matrixEnd` is `𝟙`. Proved here over the single overlap chart (no pullback types).
637	  have hB : matrixToFreeIso
638	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
639	            (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
640	          (universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI))
641	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
642	            (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
643	          (universalMinor d r I I hI hI))
644	        (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I I hI hI).1
645	        (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I I hI hI).2
646	      = Iso.refl _ := by
647	    apply Iso.ext
648	    rw [matrixToFreeIso_hom, Iso.refl_hom, universalMinorInv_self, map_one, matrixEnd_one]
649	  -- Unfold the transition, collapse the identity matrix factor (`erw` to bridge the
650	  -- `chartOverlap`/`Spec` defeq on the inferred base scheme), and cancel the comparisons.
651	  simp only [bundleTransition]
652	  erw [hB, Iso.refl_trans]
653	  exact Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso hφ (Fin d)
654	
655	/-- The bundle transition data packaged over the Grassmannian glue datum, ready to feed the
656	gluing primitive `Scheme.Modules.glue`. Project-local. -/
657	noncomputable def bundleTransitionData (d r : ℕ) :
658	    ∀ (I J : (theGlueData d r).J),
659	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).f I J)).obj

655	/-- The bundle transition data packaged over the Grassmannian glue datum, ready to feed the
656	gluing primitive `Scheme.Modules.glue`. Project-local. -/
657	noncomputable def bundleTransitionData (d r : ℕ) :
658	    ∀ (I J : (theGlueData d r).J),
659	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).f I J)).obj
660	          (SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) ≅
661	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).t I J ≫ (theGlueData d r).f J I)).obj
662	          (SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U J).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :=
663	  fun I J => bundleTransition d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
664	
665	/-! ### The matrix-level Cramer-inverse cocycle (L1)
666	
667	The matrix-algebra core of (C2) is the Cramer-inverse cocycle
668	`(X^J_K)⁻¹ (X^I_J)⁻¹ = (X^I_K)⁻¹` over the triple-overlap ring `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`.
669	Its proof reduces to the image-matrix cocycle `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` of
670	`GrassmannianCells` by taking the `I`-minor. That lemma and the matrix helpers it depends on
671	are `private` in `GrassmannianCells.lean`, so they are reproduced here as project-local
672	helpers (the proofs are verbatim ports of the known-good originals). -/
673	
674	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.mul_submatrix_col` (private there). -/
675	private lemma mul_submatrix_col' {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
676	    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
677	    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
678	  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]
679	
680	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.map_nonsing_inv` (private there). -/
681	private lemma map_nonsing_inv' {n : ℕ} {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] (f : R →+* S)
682	    (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) (h : IsUnit A.det) :
683	    (A.map f)⁻¹ = A⁻¹.map f := by
684	  have hmul : (A.map f) * (A⁻¹.map f) = 1 := by
685	    rw [← Matrix.map_mul, Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A h, Matrix.map_one f (map_zero f) (map_one f)]
686	  exact Matrix.inv_eq_right_inv hmul
687	
688	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.map_map_eq_of_comp` (private there). -/
689	private lemma map_map_eq_of_comp' {m n : ℕ} {R A D : Type*}
690	    [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [CommRing D]
691	    (M : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) R) (f : R →+* A) (g : A →+* D) (h : R →+* D)
692	    (hcomp : g.comp f = h) : (M.map f).map g = M.map h := by
693	  rw [Matrix.map_map, ← RingHom.coe_comp, hcomp]
694	
695	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.isUnit_algebraMap_away_left` (private there). -/
696	private lemma isUnit_algebraMap_away_left' {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
697	    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) x) := by
698	  have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
699	    IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
700	  rw [map_mul] at h
701	  exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_left h
702	
703	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel` (private there). -/
704	private lemma inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel' {d e : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
705	    (A B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin e) R) (hA : IsUnit A.det) :
706	    (B⁻¹ * A) * (A⁻¹ * M) = B⁻¹ * M := by
707	  rw [Matrix.mul_assoc B⁻¹ A (A⁻¹ * M), ← Matrix.mul_assoc A A⁻¹ M,
708	    Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A hA, Matrix.one_mul]
709	
710	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.imageMatrix_map_eq` (private there). -/
711	private lemma imageMatrix_map_eq' (d r : ℕ) (I X : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
712	    (hX : X.card = d) {D : Type*} [CommRing D]
713	    [Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D]
714	    (incl : Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX) →+* D)
715	    (hincl : incl.comp (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
716	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX)))
717	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D) :
718	    (imageMatrix d r I X hI hX).map incl
719	      = (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
720	            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D)).submatrix id
721	          (fun j : Fin d => (X.orderIsoOfFin hX j : Fin r)))⁻¹ *
722	        (universalMatrix d r I hI).map
723	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D) := by
724	  have hmm : (imageMatrix d r I X hI hX).map incl
725	      = (universalMinorInv d r I X hI hX).map incl
726	        * ((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
727	            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
728	              (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX)))).map incl := by
729	    rw [imageMatrix]; exact Matrix.map_mul
730	  rw [hmm, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hincl, universalMinorInv,
731	    ← map_nonsing_inv' incl (universalMinor d r I X hI hX)
732	        (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I X hI hX)]
733	  congr 1
734	  rw [universalMinor, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hincl, ← Matrix.submatrix_map]
735	
736	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` (private there): over the triple
737	overlap `S_I`, the image matrix `(X^I_K)⁻¹ X^I` of `θ_{I,K}` equals `θ_{I,J}` applied
738	entrywise to the image matrix `(X^J_K)⁻¹ X^J` of `θ_{J,K}`. -/
739	private lemma cocycle_imageMatrix_eq' (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
740	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
741	    (imageMatrix d r I K hI hK).map
742	        (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
743	      = (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).map
744	          ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
745	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))) := by
746	  have hLHS := imageMatrix_map_eq' d r I K hI hK
747	    (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
748	    (awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap _ _)
749	  have hMJimg := imageMatrix_map_eq' d r I J hI hJ
750	    (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
751	    (awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap _ _)
752	  set Y := (universalMatrix d r I hI).map
753	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
754	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK))) with hY
755	  have hYJ : IsUnit (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det := by
756	    have e : (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det
757	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
758	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK))
759	            (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) := by
760	      rw [hY, Matrix.submatrix_map]
761	      exact (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
762	    rw [e]; exact isUnit_algebraMap_away_left' _ _
763	  have hχ : ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
764	        (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))).comp
765	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
766	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
767	      = (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
768	          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom := by
769	    rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap, cocycleΘIJ]
770	    exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp _ _
771	  have hMJ : (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
772	        ((awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
773	          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
774	      = (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)))⁻¹ * Y := by
775	    have e1 : (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
776	          ((awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
777	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
778	        = (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).map
779	            (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
780	      rw [← map_map_eq_of_comp' (universalMatrix d r J hJ)
781	          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom
782	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) _ rfl]
783	      congr 1
784	      exact universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ
785	    rw [e1, hMJimg]
786	  have hRHS : (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).map
787	        ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
788	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
789	      = (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r)))⁻¹ * Y := by
790	    have hmm : (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).map
791	          ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
792	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
793	        = (universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK).map
794	            ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
795	              (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
796	          * ((universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
797	              (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
798	                (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).map
799	                  ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
800	                    (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))) := by
801	      rw [imageMatrix]; exact Matrix.map_mul
802	    rw [hmm, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hχ, hMJ, universalMinorInv,
803	      ← map_nonsing_inv' _ _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r J K hJ hK), universalMinor,
804	      map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hχ, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, hMJ,
805	      mul_submatrix_col' (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)))⁻¹ Y
806	        (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r)),
807	      Matrix.mul_inv_rev, Matrix.nonsing_inv_nonsing_inv _ hYJ,
808	      inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel' _ _ _ hYJ]
809	  rw [hLHS, hRHS]
810	
811	/-- **Cramer-inverse cocycle on the triple overlap (L1)** (`lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix`):
812	over the triple-overlap ring `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]` the base-changed Cramer inverses of
813	the localised minors satisfy the multiplicative cocycle identity
814	`(X^J_K)⁻¹ (X^I_J)⁻¹ = (X^I_K)⁻¹`. This is the pure matrix-algebra core of (C2), independent
815	of any sheaf data. Project-local. -/
816	theorem bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r))
817	    (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
818	    (universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK).map
819	        ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
820	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
821	      * (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).map
822	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
823	      = (universalMinorInv d r I K hI hK).map
824	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
825	  -- Take the `I`-minor (columns indexed by `I`) of the image-matrix cocycle.
826	  have hcol := congrArg
827	    (fun M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away
828	        (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK)) =>
829	      M.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)))
830	    (cocycle_imageMatrix_eq' d r I J K hI hJ hK)
831	  simp only at hcol
832	  -- LHS of `hcol` is `(X^I_K)⁻¹` over `S_I`.
833	  rw [Matrix.submatrix_map, imageMatrix_submatrix_I] at hcol
834	  -- RHS of `hcol`: push the `I`-minor through the outer map.
835	  rw [Matrix.submatrix_map] at hcol
836	  -- `imageMatrix J K = (X^J_K)⁻¹ * X^J`, so its `I`-minor splits off the inverse factor;
837	  -- the second factor is `X^J` (over `R^J[1/P^J_K]`) restricted to the `I`-columns.
838	  have hsplit : (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).submatrix id
839	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))
840	      = universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK *
841	        ((universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
842	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
843	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).submatrix id
844	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) := by
845	    rw [imageMatrix]; exact mul_submatrix_col' _ _ _
846	  rw [hsplit, Matrix.map_mul] at hcol
847	  -- The comp identity `θ_{I,J}` realises the cross-localisation map on `R^J`.
848	  have hχ : ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
849	        (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))).comp
850	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
851	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
852	      = (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
853	          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom := by
854	    rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap, cocycleΘIJ]
855	    exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp _ _
856	  have e1 : (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
857	        ((awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
858	          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
859	      = (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).map
860	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
861	    rw [← map_map_eq_of_comp' (universalMatrix d r J hJ)
862	        (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom
863	        (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) _ rfl]
864	    congr 1
865	    exact universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ
866	  -- The base change of `X^J|_I` over `θ_{I,J}` is `(X^I_J)⁻¹` over `S_I`.
867	  have hXJI : (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
868	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
869	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).submatrix id

module

prelude
public import Lean.Elab.MutualDef
public import Lean.Elab.MutualInductive
import Lean.Compiler.ExternAttr

public section
namespace Lean.Elab.Command

open Meta

private def ensureValidNamespace (name : Name) : MacroM Unit := do
  match name with
  | .str p s =>
    if s == "_root_" then
      Macro.throwError s!"invalid namespace `{name}`, `_root_` is a reserved namespace"
    ensureValidNamespace p
  | .num .. => Macro.throwError s!"invalid namespace `{name}`, it must not contain numeric parts"
  | .anonymous => return ()

private def setDeclIdName (declId : Syntax) (nameNew : Name) : Syntax :=
  let (id, _) := expandDeclIdCore declId
  -- We should not update the name of `def _root_.` declarations
  assert! !(`_root_).isPrefixOf id
  let idStx := mkIdent nameNew |>.raw.setInfo declId.getHeadInfo
  if declId.isIdent then
    idStx
  else
    declId.setArg 0 idStx

/-- Return `true` if `stx` is a `Command.declaration`, and it is a declaration that always has a name. -/
private def isNamedDecl (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  if !stx.isOfKind ``Lean.Parser.Command.declaration then
    false
  else
    let decl := stx[1]
    let k := decl.getKind
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.abbrev ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.definition ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.theorem ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.opaque ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.axiom ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.inductive ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.classInductive ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.structure

/-- Return `true` if `stx` is an `instance` declaration command -/
private def isInstanceDecl (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  stx.isOfKind ``Lean.Parser.Command.declaration &&
  stx[1].getKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.instance

/-- Return `some name` if `stx` is a declaration named `name` -/
private def getDeclName? (stx : Syntax) : Option Name := do
  if isNamedDecl stx then
    let (id, _) := expandDeclIdCore stx[1][1]
    some id
  else if isInstanceDecl stx then
    let optDeclId := stx[1][3]
    if optDeclId.isNone then none
    else
      let (id, _) := expandDeclIdCore optDeclId[0]
      some id
  else
    none

/--
Update the name of the given declaration.
This function assumes `stx` is not a nameless instance.
-/
private def setDeclName (stx : Syntax) (name : Name) : Syntax :=
  if isNamedDecl stx then
    stx.setArg 1 <| stx[1].setArg 1 <| setDeclIdName stx[1][1] name
  else if isInstanceDecl stx then
    -- We never set the name of nameless instance declarations
    assert! !stx[1][3].isNone
    stx.setArg 1 <| stx[1].setArg 3 <| stx[1][3].setArg 0 <| setDeclIdName stx[1][3][0] name
  else
    stx

/--
  Given declarations such as `@[...] def Foo.Bla.f ...` return `some (Foo.Bla, @[...] def f ...)`
  Remark: if the id starts with `_root_`, we return `none`.
-/
private def expandDeclNamespace? (stx : Syntax) : MacroM (Option (Name × Syntax)) := do
  let some name := getDeclName? stx | return none
  if (`_root_).isPrefixOf name then
    ensureValidNamespace (name.replacePrefix `_root_ Name.anonymous)
    return none
  let scpView := extractMacroScopes name
  match scpView.name with
  | .str .anonymous _ => return none
  | .str pre shortName => return some (pre, setDeclName stx { scpView with name := .mkSimple shortName }.review)
  | _ => return none

def elabAxiom (modifiers : Modifiers) (stx : Syntax) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  -- leading_parser "axiom " >> declId >> declSig
  let declId             := stx[1]
  let (binders, typeStx) := expandDeclSig stx[2]
  runTermElabM fun vars => do
    let scopeLevelNames ← Term.getLevelNames
    let ⟨shortName, declName, allUserLevelNames, docString?⟩ ← Term.expandDeclId (← getCurrNamespace) scopeLevelNames declId modifiers
    addDeclarationRangesForBuiltin declName modifiers.stx stx
    Term.withAutoBoundImplicit do
    Term.withAutoBoundImplicitForbiddenPred (fun n => shortName == n) do
    Term.withDeclName declName <| Term.withLevelNames allUserLevelNames <| Term.elabBinders binders.getArgs fun xs => do
      Term.applyAttributesAt declName modifiers.attrs AttributeApplicationTime.beforeElaboration
      let type ← Term.elabType typeStx
      Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing
      let xs ← Term.addAutoBoundImplicits xs (declId.getTailPos? (canonicalOnly := true))
      let type ← instantiateMVars type
      let type ← mkForallFVars xs type
      let type ← mkForallFVars vars type (usedOnly := true)
      let type ← Term.levelMVarToParam type
      let usedParams  := collectLevelParams {} type |>.params
      match sortDeclLevelParams scopeLevelNames allUserLevelNames usedParams with
      | Except.error msg      => throwErrorAt stx msg
      | Except.ok levelParams =>
        let type ← instantiateMVars type
        let decl := Declaration.axiomDecl {
          name        := declName,
          levelParams := levelParams,
          type        := type,
          isUnsafe    := modifiers.isUnsafe
        }
        trace[Elab.axiom] "{declName} : {type}"
        Term.ensureNoUnassignedMVars decl
        addDecl decl

        Term.applyAttributesAt declName modifiers.attrs AttributeApplicationTime.afterTypeChecking
        if isExtern (← getEnv) declName then
          compileDecl decl
        if let some (doc, isVerso) := docString? then
          addDocStringOf isVerso declName binders doc
        Term.applyAttributesAt declName modifiers.attrs AttributeApplicationTime.afterCompilation
        withSaveInfoContext do  -- save new env with docstring and decl
          Term.addTermInfo' declId (← mkConstWithLevelParams declName) (isBinder := true)
        enableRealizationsForConst declName
open Lean.Parser.Command.InternalSyntax in
/--
Macro that expands a declaration with a complex name into an explicit `namespace` block.
Implementing this step as a macro means that reuse checking is handled by `elabCommand`.
 -/
@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.declaration]
def expandNamespacedDeclaration : Macro := fun stx => do
  match (← expandDeclNamespace? stx) with
  | some (ns, newStx) => do
    -- Limit ref variability for incrementality; see Note [Incremental Macros]
    let declTk := stx[1][0]
    let ns := mkIdentFrom declTk ns
    withRef declTk `(namespace $ns $endLocalScopeSyntax:command $(⟨newStx⟩) end $ns)
  | none => Macro.throwUnsupported

@[builtin_command_elab declaration, builtin_incremental]
def elabDeclaration : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  withExporting (isExporting := (← getScope).isPublic) do
  let modifiers : TSyntax ``Parser.Command.declModifiers := ⟨stx[0]⟩
  let decl     := stx[1]
  let declKind := decl.getKind
  if isDefLike decl then
    -- only case implementing incrementality currently
    elabMutualDef #[stx]
  else withoutCommandIncrementality true do
    -- use hash of declaration name, if any, as stable quot context; `elabMutualDef` has its own
    -- handling
    withInitQuotContext (getDeclName? stx |>.map hash) do
    let mut modifiers ← elabModifiers modifiers
    if (← getScope).isMeta && modifiers.computeKind == .regular then
      modifiers := { modifiers with computeKind := .meta }
    withExporting (isExporting := modifiers.isInferredPublic (← getEnv)) do
      if declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«axiom» then
        elabAxiom modifiers decl
      else if declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«inductive»
          || declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«coinductive»
          || declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.classInductive
          || declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«structure» then
        elabInductive modifiers decl
      else
        throwError "unexpected declaration"

/-- Return true if all elements of the mutual-block are definitions/theorems/abbrevs. -/
private def isMutualDefLike (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  stx[1].getArgs.all fun elem =>
    let decl := elem[1]
    isDefLike decl

private def isMutualPreambleCommand (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  let k := stx.getKind
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.variable ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.universe ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.check ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.set_option ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.open

private partial def splitMutualPreamble (elems : Array Syntax) : Option (Array Syntax × Array Syntax) :=
  let rec loop (i : Nat) : Option (Array Syntax × Array Syntax) :=
    if h : i < elems.size then
      if isMutualPreambleCommand elems[i] then
        loop (i+1)
      else if i == 0 then
        none -- `mutual` block does not contain any preamble commands
      else
        some (elems[*...i], elems[i...elems.size])
    else
      none -- a `mutual` block containing only preamble commands is not a valid `mutual` block
  loop 0

/--
Find the common namespace for the given names.
Example:
```
findCommonPrefix [`Lean.Elab.eval, `Lean.mkConst, `Lean.Elab.Tactic.evalTactic]
-- `Lean
```
-/
def findCommonPrefix (ns : List Name) : Name :=
  match ns with
  | [] => .anonymous
  | n :: ns => go n ns
where
  go (n : Name) (ns : List Name) : Name :=
    match n with
    | .anonymous => .anonymous
    | _ => match ns with
      | [] => n
      | n' :: ns => go (findCommon n.components n'.components) ns
  findCommon (as bs : List Name) : Name :=
    match as, bs with
    | a :: as, b :: bs => if a == b then a ++ findCommon as bs else .anonymous
    | _, _ => .anonymous


@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.mutual]
def expandMutualNamespace : Macro := fun stx => do
  let mut nss := #[]
  for elem in stx[1].getArgs do
    match (← expandDeclNamespace? elem) with
    | none        => Macro.throwUnsupported
    | some (n, _) => nss := nss.push n
  let common := findCommonPrefix nss.toList
  if common.isAnonymous then Macro.throwUnsupported
  let elemsNew ← stx[1].getArgs.mapM fun elem => do
    let some name := getDeclName? elem | unreachable!
    let view := extractMacroScopes name
    let nameNew := { view with name := view.name.replacePrefix common .anonymous }.review
    return setDeclName elem nameNew
  let ns := mkIdentFrom stx common
  let stxNew := stx.setArg 1 (mkNullNode elemsNew)
  `(namespace $ns $(⟨stxNew⟩) end $ns)

@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.mutual]
def expandMutualElement : Macro := fun stx => do
  let mut elemsNew := #[]
  let mut modified := false
  for elem in stx[1].getArgs do
    -- Don't trigger the `expandNamespacedDecl` macro, the namespace is handled by the mutual def
    -- elaborator directly instead
    if !elem.isOfKind ``Parser.Command.declaration then
      if let some elemNew ← expandMacro? elem then
        if elemNew.isOfKind nullKind then
          elemsNew := elemsNew ++ elemNew.getArgs
        else
          elemsNew := elemsNew.push elemNew
        modified := true
        continue
    elemsNew := elemsNew.push elem
  if modified then
    return stx.setArg 1 (mkNullNode elemsNew)
  else
    Macro.throwUnsupported

@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.mutual]
def expandMutualPreamble : Macro := fun stx =>
  match splitMutualPreamble stx[1].getArgs with
  | none => Macro.throwUnsupported
  | some (preamble, rest) => do
    let secCmd    ← `(section)
    let newMutual := stx.setArg 1 (mkNullNode rest)
    let endCmd    ← `(end)
    return mkNullNode (#[secCmd] ++ preamble ++ #[newMutual] ++ #[endCmd])

@[builtin_command_elab «mutual», builtin_incremental]
def elabMutual : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  withExporting (isExporting := (← getScope).isPublic) do
  if isMutualDefLike stx then
    -- only case implementing incrementality currently
    elabMutualDef stx[1].getArgs
  else withoutCommandIncrementality true do
    if ← isMutualInductive stx then
      elabMutualInductive stx[1].getArgs
    else
      throwError "invalid mutual block: either all elements of the block must be inductive/structure declarations, or they must all be definitions/theorems/abbrevs"

/- leading_parser "attribute " >> "[" >> sepBy1 (eraseAttr <|> Term.attrInstance) ", " >> "]" >> many1 ident -/
@[builtin_command_elab «attribute»] def elabAttr : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let mut attrInsts := #[]
  let mut toErase := #[]
  for attrKindStx in stx[2].getSepArgs do
    if attrKindStx.getKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.eraseAttr then
      let attrName := attrKindStx[1].getId.eraseMacroScopes
      if isAttribute (← getEnv) attrName then
        toErase := toErase.push attrName
      else
        logErrorAt attrKindStx m!"unknown attribute [{attrName}]"
    else
      attrInsts := attrInsts.push attrKindStx
  let attrs ← elabAttrs attrInsts
  let idents := stx[4].getArgs
  for ident in idents do withRef ident <| liftTermElabM do
    /-
    HACK to allow `attribute` command to disable builtin simprocs.
    TODO: find a better solution. Example: have some "fake" declaration
    for builtin simprocs.
    -/
    let declNames ←
      try
        realizeGlobalConstWithInfos ident
      catch _ =>
        let name := ident.getId.eraseMacroScopes
        if (← Simp.isBuiltinSimproc name) then
          pure [name]
        else
          throwUnknownConstantAt ident name
    let declName ← ensureNonAmbiguous ident declNames
    withExporting (isExporting := !isPrivateName declName && attrs.any (·.kind != .local)) do
      recordExtraModUseFromDecl (isMeta := false) declName
    Term.applyAttributes declName attrs
    for attrName in toErase do
      Attribute.erase declName attrName
    if (← getEnv).isImportedConst declName then
      if let some attr := attrs.find? (·.kind != .local) then
        -- If an imported declaration is marked with a non-local attribute, Shake must be informed
        -- about this indirect reference
        recordIndirectModUse attr.name.toString declName

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.Parser.Command.«initialize»] def elabInitialize : CommandElab
  | stx@`($declModifiers:declModifiers $kw:initializeKeyword $[$id? : $type? ←]? $doSeq) => do
    let attrId := mkIdentFrom stx <| if kw.raw[0].isToken "initialize" then `init else `builtin_init
    if let (some id, some type) := (id?, type?) then
      let `(Parser.Command.declModifiersT| $[$doc?:docComment]? $[@[$attrs?,*]]? $(vis?)? $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]?) := stx[0]
        | throwErrorAt declModifiers "invalid initialization command, unexpected modifiers"
      let defStx ← `($[$doc?:docComment]? @[$attrId:ident initFn, $(attrs?.getD ∅),*] $(vis?)? $[meta%$meta?]? opaque $id : $type)
      let mut fullId := (← getCurrNamespace) ++ id.getId
      if vis?.any (·.raw.isOfKind ``Parser.Command.private) then
        fullId := mkPrivateName (← getEnv) fullId
      -- We need to add `id`'s ranges *before* elaborating `initFn` (and then `id` itself) as
      -- otherwise the info context created by `with_decl_name` will be incomplete and break the
      -- call hierarchy
      addDeclarationRangesForBuiltin fullId ⟨defStx.raw[0]⟩ defStx.raw[1]
      let vis := Parser.Command.visibility.ofBool (!isPrivateName fullId)
      elabCommand (← `(
        $vis:visibility $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]? def initFn : IO $type := with_decl_name% $(mkIdent fullId) do $doSeq
        $defStx:command))
    else
      let `(Parser.Command.declModifiersT| $[$doc?:docComment]? $[@[$attrs?,*]]? $(_)? $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]?) := declModifiers
        | throwErrorAt declModifiers "invalid initialization command, unexpected modifiers"
      let attrs := (attrs?.map (·.getElems)).getD #[]
      let attrs := attrs.push (← `(Lean.Parser.Term.attrInstance| $attrId:ident))
      -- `[builtin_init]` can be private as it is used for local codegen only but `[init]` must be
      -- available for the interpreter.
      let vis := Parser.Command.visibility.ofBool (attrId.getId == `init)
      elabCommand (← `($[$doc?:docComment]? @[$[$attrs],*] $vis:visibility $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]? def initFn : IO Unit := do $doSeq))
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

builtin_initialize
  registerTraceClass `Elab.axiom

end Lean.Elab.Command

module

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Free
public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Preserves.Shapes.Products
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.Final.Type

/-!
# Pullbacks of free sheaves of modules

Let `S` (resp.`R`) be a sheaf of rings on a category `C` (resp. `D`)
equipped with a Grothendieck topology `J` (resp. `K`).
Let `F : C ⥤ D` be a continuous functor.
Let `φ` be a morphism from `S` to the direct image of `R`.

We introduce `unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ` which is the morphism
in the category `SheafOfModules S` which corresponds to `φ`, and
show that the adjoint morphism
`pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ : (pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R`
is an isomorphism when `F` is a final functor.
More generally, the functor `pullback φ` sends the free sheaf
of modules `free I` to `free I`, see `pullbackObjFreeIso` and
`freeFunctorCompPullbackIso`.
-/

@[expose] public section

universe v v₁ v₂ u₁ u₂ u

open CategoryTheory Limits

namespace SheafOfModules

variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
  {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {K : GrothendieckTopology D} {F : C ⥤ D}
  {S : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf K RingCat.{u}}
  [Functor.IsContinuous F J K]
  (φ : S ⟶ (F.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat.{u} J K).obj R)

/-- The canonical map from the (global) sections of a sheaf of modules
to the (global) sections of its pushforward. -/
@[simps]
def pushforwardSections {M : SheafOfModules.{v} R} (s : M.sections) :
    ((pushforward φ).obj M).sections where
  val _ := s.val _
  property _ := s.property _

variable (M) in
lemma bijective_pushforwardSections [F.Final] :
    Function.Bijective (pushforwardSections φ (M := M)) :=
  Functor.bijective_sectionsPrecomp _ _

variable [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
  [K.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]

/-- The canonical morphism `unit S ⟶ (pushforward.{u} φ).obj (unit R)`
of sheaves of modules corresponding to a continuous map between ringed sites. -/
noncomputable def unitToPushforwardObjUnit : unit S ⟶ (pushforward.{u} φ).obj (unit R) where
  val.app X := ModuleCat.homMk ((forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat).map (φ.hom.app X)) (fun r ↦ by
    ext m
    exact ((φ.hom.app X).hom.map_mul _ _).symm)
  val.naturality f := by
    ext
    exact ConcreteCategory.congr_hom (φ.hom.naturality f) _

lemma unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply {X : Cᵒᵖ} (a : S.obj.obj X) :
    (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ).val.app X a = φ.hom.app X a := rfl

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
lemma pushforwardSections_unitHomEquiv
    {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : unit R ⟶ M) :
    pushforwardSections φ (M.unitHomEquiv f) =
      ((pushforward φ).obj M).unitHomEquiv
        (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ ≫ (pushforward φ).map f) := by
  ext X
  have := unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply φ (X := X) 1
  dsimp at this ⊢
  simp +instances [this, map_one]
  rfl

variable [(pushforward.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint]

/-- The canonical morphism `(pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R`
of sheaves of modules corresponding to a continuous map between ringed sites. -/
noncomputable def pullbackObjUnitToUnit :
    (pullback.{u} φ).obj (unit S) ⟶ unit R :=
  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).symm (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ)

@[simp]
lemma pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_symm_unitToPushforwardObjUnit :
    ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).symm (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ) =
      pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit :
    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _ (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ) =
      unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ :=
  Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _

instance [F.Final] : IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ) := by
  rw [isIso_iff_coyoneda_map_bijective]
  intro M
  rw [← ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{u} φ).homEquiv _ _).bijective.of_comp_iff',
    ← (unitHomEquiv _).bijective.of_comp_iff']
  convert (bijective_pushforwardSections φ M).comp (unitHomEquiv _).bijective
  ext f : 1
  dsimp

    Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right,

variable [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [HasWeakSheafify K AddCommGrpCat.{u}]

  [K.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [F.Final]

/-- The pullback of a free sheaf of modules is a free sheaf of modules. -/
noncomputable def pullbackObjFreeIso (I : Type u) :
    (pullback φ).obj (free I) ≅ free I :=
  (asIso (sigmaComparison _ _)).symm ≪≫
    Sigma.mapIso (fun _ ↦ asIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ))

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom {I : Type u} (i : I) :
    (pullback φ).map (ιFree i) ≫ (pullbackObjFreeIso φ I).hom =
      pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ ≫ ιFree i := by
  simp [pullbackObjFreeIso, ιFree]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma pullbackObjFreeIso_hom_naturality {I J : Type u} (f : I → J) :
    (pullback φ).map (freeMap f) ≫ (pullbackObjFreeIso φ J).hom =
      (pullbackObjFreeIso φ I).hom ≫ freeMap f :=
  Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit (pullback φ) _ _
    (isColimitFreeCofan (R := S) I)) _ _ (fun i ↦ by simp [← Functor.map_comp_assoc])

/-- The canonical isomorphism `freeFunctor ⋙ pullback φ ≅ freeFunctor` for a
continuous map between ringed sites, when the underlying functor between the sites
is final. -/
noncomputable def freeFunctorCompPullbackIso : freeFunctor ⋙ pullback φ ≅ freeFunctor :=
  NatIso.ofComponents (fun X ↦ pullbackObjFreeIso φ X)

end SheafOfModules

module

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf
public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Whiskering

/-!
# The category of presheaves of modules over a scheme

In this file, given a scheme `X`, we define the category of presheaves
of modules over `X`. As categories of presheaves of modules are
defined for presheaves of rings (and not presheaves of commutative rings),
we also introduce a definition `X.ringCatSheaf` for the underlying sheaf
of rings of `X`.

-/

@[expose] public section

universe u

open CategoryTheory

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

variable (X Y : Scheme.{u})

/-- The underlying sheaf of rings of a scheme. -/
abbrev ringCatSheaf : TopCat.Sheaf RingCat.{u} X :=
  (sheafCompose _ (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat.{u})).obj X.sheaf

/-- The category of presheaves of modules over a scheme. -/
nonrec abbrev PresheafOfModules := PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj

variable {X Y} in
/-- The morphism of sheaves of rings corresponding to a morphism of schemes. -/
def Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (f : X ⟶ Y) :
    Y.ringCatSheaf ⟶ ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).sheafPushforwardContinuous
      _ _ _).obj X.ringCatSheaf where
  hom := Functor.whiskerRight f.c _

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

/-- The structure sheaf of a scheme. -/
protected abbrev sheaf (X : Scheme) :=
  X.toSheafedSpace.sheaf

/--
We give schemes the specialization preorder by default.
-/
instance {X : Scheme.{u}} : Preorder X := specializationPreorder X

lemma le_iff_specializes {X : Scheme.{u}} {a b : X} : a ≤ b ↔ b ⤳ a := by rfl

open Order in
lemma height_of_isClosed {X : Scheme} {x : X} (hx : IsClosed {x}) : height x = 0 := by
  simp only [height_eq_zero]
  intro b _
  obtain rfl | h := eq_or_ne b x
  · assumption
  · have := IsClosed.not_specializes hx rfl h
    contradiction

namespace Hom

variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) {U U' : Y.Opens} {V V' : X.Opens}

/-- Given a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y`, and open `U ⊆ Y`,
this is the induced map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ U)`.

This is treated as a suffix in lemma names. -/
abbrev app (U : Y.Opens) : Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) :=
  f.c.app (op U)

/-- Given a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y`, this is the induced map `Γ(Y, ⊤) ⟶ Γ(X, ⊤)`.
This is treated as a suffix in lemma names. -/
abbrev appTop : Γ(Y, ⊤) ⟶ Γ(X, ⊤) :=
  f.app ⊤

@[reassoc]
lemma naturality (i : op U' ⟶ op U) :
    Y.presheaf.map i ≫ f.app U = f.app U' ≫ X.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i.unop).op :=
  f.c.naturality i

/-- Given a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y`, and open sets `U ⊆ Y`, `V ⊆ f ⁻¹' U`,
this is the induced map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)`.

This is treated as a suffix in lemma names. -/
def appLE (U : Y.Opens) (V : X.Opens) (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) : Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V) :=
  f.app U ≫ X.presheaf.map (homOfLE e).op

lemma appLE_map (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U) (i : op V ⟶ op V') :

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (h : Z ⟶ T)

/-- The pushforward functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pushforward : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_obj (M : X.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_presheaf_map {U V : Y.Opens} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    ((pushforward f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_map_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforward f).map φ).app U = φ.app (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pullback : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ⊣ pushforward f :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _

section

attribute [local instance] preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts
  preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryProducts

instance : (pullback f).IsLeftAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).IsRightAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isRightAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
instance : (pullback f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _

end

/-! ### L3 transport: `scalarEnd`/`matrixEnd` naturality under pullback

The substantive new infrastructure for (C2). The atom is `scalarEnd_pullback`: pulling back a
scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` along a scheme morphism `p` is, after the unit-pullback
comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`, the scalar endomorphism of the base-changed function
`p.appTop a`. Its proof transposes the naturality square under the pullback-pushforward
adjunction to a `unit`-level identity, which holds by naturality of the comorphism `p.c`. -/

/-- The reduced (transposed) form of the scalar-naturality atom: on the unit sheaf,
multiplication by `a` followed by the comorphism `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` equals the
comorphism followed by the pushforward of multiplication by `p.appTop a`. Project-local
helper for `scalarEnd_pullback`. -/
lemma unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm {T S : Scheme.{u}} (p : T ⟶ S) (a : Γ(S, ⊤)) :
    scalarEnd a ≫ SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) ≫
        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map (scalarEnd (p.appTop a)) := by
  apply (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.injective
  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
  sorry

/-- **ATOM: scalar endomorphism naturality under pullback** (`lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback`).
For `p : T ⟶ S` and `a ∈ Γ(S,⊤)`, pulling back the scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` is,
after the unit-pullback comparison `q = pullbackObjUnitToUnit`, the scalar endomorphism of the
base-changed function `p.appTop a`:
`(pullback p).map (scalarEnd a) ≫ q = q ≫ scalarEnd (p.appTop a)`.
Proved by transposing under the pullback-pushforward adjunction to `unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm`.
Project-local — the single irreducible new claim underlying `matrixEnd_pullback`. -/
lemma scalarEnd_pullback {T S : Scheme.{u}} (p : T ⟶ S) (a : Γ(S, ⊤)) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (scalarEnd a) ≫
        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
      = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) ≫
        scalarEnd (p.appTop a) := by
  apply (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).homEquiv
    (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf) |>.injective
  rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
  rw [show (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).homEquiv
        (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)
        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p))
      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) from
    SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit _]
  exact unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm p a

@[simp]
lemma hom_comp {M N O : ModuleCat.{v} R} (f : M ⟶ N) (g : N ⟶ O) :
    (f ≫ g).hom = g.hom.comp f.hom := rfl

/- Provided for rewriting. -/
lemma comp_apply {M N O : ModuleCat.{v} R} (f : M ⟶ N) (g : N ⟶ O) (x : M) :
    (f ≫ g) x = g (f x) := by simp

@[ext]
lemma hom_ext {M N : ModuleCat.{v} R} {f g : M ⟶ N} (hf : f.hom = g.hom) : f = g :=
  Hom.ext hf

/-- The type of sections of a presheaf of modules. -/
def sections (M : PresheafOfModules.{v} R) : Type _ := (M.presheaf ⋙ forget _).sections

/-- Given a presheaf of modules `M`, `s : M.sections` and `X : Cᵒᵖ`, this is the induced
element in `M.obj X`. -/
abbrev sections.eval {M : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} (s : M.sections) (X : Cᵒᵖ) : M.obj X := s.1 X

@[simp]
lemma sections_property {M : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} (s : M.sections)
    {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) : M.map f (s.1 X) = s.1 Y := s.2 f

/-- Constructor for sections of a presheaf of modules. -/
@[simps]
def sectionsMk {M : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} (s : ∀ X, M.obj X)
    (hs : ∀ ⦃X Y : Cᵒᵖ⦄ (f : X ⟶ Y), M.map f (s X) = s Y) : M.sections where
  val := s
  property f := hs f

@[ext]
lemma sections_ext {M : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} (s t : M.sections)
    (h : ∀ (X : Cᵒᵖ), s.val X = t.val X) : s = t :=
  Subtype.ext (by ext; apply h)

/-- The map `M.sections → N.sections` induced by a morphisms `M ⟶ N` of presheaves of modules. -/
@[simps!]
def sectionsMap {M N : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} (f : M ⟶ N) (s : M.sections) : N.sections :=
  N.sectionsMk (fun X ↦ f.app X (s.1 _))
    (fun X Y g ↦ by rw [← naturality_apply, sections_property])

@[simp]
lemma sectionsMap_comp {M N P : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} (f : M ⟶ N) (g : N ⟶ P) (s : M.sections) :
    sectionsMap (f ≫ g) s = sectionsMap g (sectionsMap f s) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma sectionsMap_id {M : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} (s : M.sections) :
    sectionsMap (𝟙 M) s = s := rfl

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in

noncomputable def unitHomEquiv (M : PresheafOfModules R) :
    (unit R ⟶ M) ≃ M.sections where
  toFun f := sectionsMk (fun X ↦ Hom.app f X (1 : R.obj X))

theorem congr_hom {X Y : C} {f g : X ⟶ Y} (h : f = g) (x : ToType X) : f x = g x :=
  congrFun (congrArg (fun k : X ⟶ Y => (k : ToType X → ToType Y)) h) x

theorem coe_id {X : C} : (𝟙 X : ToType X → ToType X) = id := by
  ext
  simp [ConcreteCategory.id_apply]

theorem coe_comp {X Y Z : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) : (f ≫ g : ToType X → ToType Z) = g ∘ f := by
  ext
  simp [ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]

    𝟙 X x = x := by
  simp [ConcreteCategory.id_apply _]

set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
set_option backward.privateInPublic.warn false in
instance moduleCategory : Category.{v, max (v + 1) u} (ModuleCat.{v} R) where
  Hom M N := Hom M N
  id _ := ⟨LinearMap.id⟩
  comp f g := ⟨g.hom'.comp f.hom'⟩

set_option backward.privateInPublic true in
set_option backward.privateInPublic.warn false in
instance : ConcreteCategory (ModuleCat.{v} R) (· →ₗ[R] ·) where
  hom := Hom.hom'
  ofHom := Hom.mk

section

variable {R}

/-- Turn a morphism in `ModuleCat` back into a `LinearMap`. -/
abbrev Hom.hom {A B : ModuleCat.{v} R} (f : Hom A B) :=
  ConcreteCategory.hom (C := ModuleCat R) f

/-- Typecheck a `LinearMap` as a morphism in `ModuleCat`. -/
abbrev ofHom {X Y : Type v} [AddCommGroup X] [Module R X] [AddCommGroup Y] [Module R Y]
    (f : X →ₗ[R] Y) : of R X ⟶ of R Y :=
  ConcreteCategory.ofHom (C := ModuleCat R) f

/-- Use the `ConcreteCategory.hom` projection for `@[simps]` lemmas. -/
def Hom.Simps.hom (A B : ModuleCat.{v} R) (f : Hom A B) :=
  f.hom

initialize_simps_projections Hom (hom' → hom)

The results below duplicate the `ConcreteCategory` simp lemmas, but we can keep them for `dsimp`.

lemma hom_id {M : ModuleCat.{v} R} : (𝟙 M : M ⟶ M).hom = LinearMap.id := rfl

content:
PresheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} (F.op ⋙ R) where
  obj M := pushforward₀_obj F R M
  map {M₁ M₂} φ := { app X := φ.app _ }

/-- If `F : C ⥤ D` if a functor and `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat` is a presheaf
of commutative rings, this is the pushforward functor from the category
of presheaves of modules on `R` to the category of presheaves of
modules on `F.op ⋙ R`. -/
abbrev pushforward₀OfCommRingCat (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}) :
    PresheafOfModules.{v} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _) ⥤
      PresheafOfModules.{v} ((F.op ⋙ R) ⋙ forget₂ _ _) :=
  pushforward₀ F (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)

/-- The pushforward of presheaves of modules commutes with the forgetful functor
to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
noncomputable def pushforward₀CompToPresheaf (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
    pushforward₀.{v} F R ⋙ toPresheaf _ ≅ toPresheaf _ ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj F.op :=
  Iso.refl _

variable {F}
variable {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)

attribute [local simp] pushforward₀ in
/-- The pushforward functor `PresheafOfModules R ⥤ PresheafOfModules S` induced by
a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`. -/
@[simps! obj_obj]
noncomputable def pushforward : PresheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} S :=
  pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ

/-- The pushforward of presheaves of modules commutes with the forgetful functor
to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
noncomputable def pushforwardCompToPresheaf :
    pushforward.{v} φ ⋙ toPresheaf _ ≅ toPresheaf _ ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj F.op :=
  Iso.refl _

lemma pushforward_obj_map_apply (M : PresheafOfModules.{v} R) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
    (m : (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X).hom).obj (M.obj (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)))) :
      (((pushforward φ).obj M).map f).hom m = M.map (F.map f.unop).op m := rfl

/-- `@[simp]`-normal form of `pushforward_obj_map_apply`. -/
@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_map_apply' (M : PresheafOfModules.{v} R) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
    (m : (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X).hom).obj (M.obj (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)))) :
      DFunLike.coe
        (F := ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _) →ₗ[_]
          ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars (S.map f).hom).obj ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _)))
        (((pushforward φ).obj M).map f).hom m = M.map (F.map f.unop).op m := rfl

lemma pushforward_map_app_apply {M N : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} (α : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
    (m : (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X).hom).obj (M.obj (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)))) :
    (((pushforward φ).map α).app X).hom m = α.app (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)) m := rfl

/-- `@[simp]`-normal form of `pushforward_map_app_apply`. -/
@[simp]
lemma pushforward_map_app_apply' {M N : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} (α : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
    (m : (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X).hom).obj (M.obj (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)))) :
    DFunLike.coe
      (F := ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _) →ₗ[_] ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _))
      (((pushforward φ).map α).app X).hom m = α.app (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)) m := rfl

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean

## Summary

- **Sorry count: 4 → 5** (added 1 scaffold), BUT added **2 fully-proven axiom-clean lemmas**
  (the L3 ATOM + its helper). The headline: **the hard-gate ATOM `scalarEnd_pullback` is CLOSED
  axiom-clean** (`lean_verify`: only `propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound`, no `sorryAx`).
- **Closed (new, axiom-clean):**
  - `unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm` — the transposed `unit`-level identity (helper for the atom).
  - `scalarEnd_pullback` — **the ATOM** (`lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback`): scalar-endomorphism
    naturality under pullback. This was the single irreducible new claim flagged by the
    progress-critic hard gate ("attack the atom first; if it stalls escalate"). It did NOT stall.
- **New scaffold (typed signature + genuine partial proof):**
  - `matrixEnd_pullback` (`lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback`, (a)) — signature pinned; proof reduced to
    the naturality square + `matrixEnd` unfold; `sorry` at the biproduct-matrix transport.
- **Still open (unchanged from iter start):** `bundleTransition_cocycle` (C2), `universalQuotient`,
  `tautologicalQuotient`, `represents`.
- Build: `lake build` clean, **25s, no OOM / no kernel timeout** (validated with real build, not
  just LSP).

## scalarEnd_pullback (the ATOM) — RESOLVED

### Approach
Transpose the naturality square under the pullback–pushforward adjunction
`Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p`. `homEquiv` of the LHS collapses by
`homEquiv_naturality_left` to `scalarEnd a ≫ unitToPushforwardObjUnit`; the RHS by
`homEquiv_naturality_right` to `unitToPushforwardObjUnit ≫ (pushforward p).map (scalarEnd (p.appTop a))`.
The two are equal by the helper `unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm`.

### Key insights / gotchas (record for reuse)
- **Universe trap:** state the atom + helper at `{T S : Scheme.{0}}`, NOT `Scheme.{u}`. At
  universe `u` the `DFunLike.coe.{u+1,…}` on the sections type does not line up with the
  `unitHomEquiv_comp_apply` lemma (which fixes universe 1); the C2/bundle context is all
  `Scheme.{0}` anyway.
- **`unitHomEquiv` takes the CODOMAIN as its explicit arg** — use
  `((pushforward p).obj (unit T)).unitHomEquiv.injective`, not `(unit S).unitHomEquiv`.
- **Diamond — `homEquiv_naturality_right` positional `rw` fails** (the value-category `≫` /
  `homEquiv` is an opaque-functor diamond): supply it as a **term** via `.trans`/`congrArg`
  (memory `grquot-functor-dropped-termmode`). Likewise `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
  is stated for `SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` (defeq but not syntactic to
  `Scheme.Modules.…`) — capture it as a `have hq` in the goal's `Scheme.Modules` form first.

## unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm — RESOLVED

### Approach
`apply ((pushforward p).obj (unit T)).unitHomEquiv.injective` then `sections_ext`. The crucial
move: the morphism composites `f ≫ g` in the value ModuleCat are an **opaque-functor diamond** that
NO positional `rw`/`simp` (`ModuleCat.hom_comp`, `CategoryTheory.comp_apply`, `LinearMap.comp_apply`)
can break — they fail asymmetrically (one side reduces, the other is in `LinearMap.instFunLike`
form vs `ToType` form). **Bypass via `change`** (defeq): since
`unitHomEquiv (f ≫ p) = sectionsMap p (unitHomEquiv f)` and `sectionsMap`/`unitHomEquiv` are
`rfl`/`sectionsMk`-defined, `change` the `.val Y` of each side into the nested-application form
`(uTPU).val.app Y ((scalarEnd a).val.app Y 1)` with the `1`s ascribed `(1 : S.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)`
(the module-carrier `1` fails `OfNat` synthesis; the ring `1` succeeds and is defeq).
Then `scalarEnd_val_app_one` + `unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply` + `map_one` reduce to the
comorphism naturality `(toRingCatSheafHom p).hom.app Y (a|_Y) = (pushforward …) 1`, closed by
`ConcreteCategory.congr_hom ((toRingCatSheafHom p).hom.naturality (homOfLE le_top).op) a`
(after `comp_apply` cleanup) and `(scalarEnd_val_app_one …).symm` (the RHS pushforward is `rfl`-defeq
to `scalarEnd` at the preimage open; the two `homOfLE ≤ ⊤` agree by proof irrelevance).

### Dead ends (do not retry)
- `apply (unit S).unitHomEquiv.injective` — wrong (M is the codomain).
- `ModuleCat.comp_apply` / `CategoryTheory.comp_apply` / `LinearMap.comp_apply` / `ModuleCat.hom_comp`
  to break the value-ModuleCat composite — all fail under the diamond (asymmetric coercion forms).
  The `change`-to-nested-application route is the one that works.

## matrixEnd_pullback (a) — PARTIAL

Signature pinned (`(pullback p).map (matrixEnd M) = Q.hom ≫ matrixEnd (p.appTop.mapMatrix M) ≫ Q.inv`,
`Q = pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)`). Proof body reduces to the naturality square
(`rw [← Category.assoc, Iso.eq_comp_inv]`) and unfolds `matrixEnd` into its biproduct presentation.
**Remaining:** distribute `pullback p` over the unfolded composite (term-mode `(pullback p).map_comp`
— positional `Functor.map_comp` hits the `X.Modules` functor diamond), then transport
`biproduct.matrix (scalarEnd ∘ M)` across `Q`/`biproduct.isoCoproduct`, reducing each `(p,q)`-entry to
`scalarEnd_pullback (M p q)`. Tooling: `biproduct.hom_ext'` / cofan `hom_ext` +
`pullbackObjFreeIso_hom_naturality`, all term-mode.

## Next concrete steps (iter-063)
1. Finish `matrixEnd_pullback` (the biproduct transport, reducing per-entry to the now-proven ATOM).
2. Build (b) `baseChange_bridge` (ΓSpecIso identification of the glue-data base-change maps with
   `awayInclLeft`/`awayInclRight`/`cocycleΘIJ`) — forward-pinned in the blueprint.
3. L3 `bundleTransition_cocycle_transport` (pure assembly: a/b + L1 + L2) → close C2.

## Why I stopped
**Real progress:** closed 2 axiom-clean lemmas — `scalarEnd_pullback` (the ATOM, the iter's
make-or-break hard gate) and its helper `unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm`. Both verified axiom-clean.
Added `matrixEnd_pullback` with a pinned signature and a genuine partial proof (naturality square +
`matrixEnd` unfold; `sorry` only at the biproduct transport). The ATOM did NOT stall — the progress-
critic's escalation branch is not triggered; the L3 chain is now unblocked at its irreducible core.
I stopped before fully closing (a)/(b)/L3/C2 because each is substantial additional work and the
atom (the gated deliverable) consumed the session's effort; the remaining steps now rest on a proven
foundation rather than the previously-missing scalar-naturality claim.
